en: On one occasion, while the crowd was pressing in on him to hear the word of God, he was standing by the lake of Gennesaret,
zh: 耶穌站在革尼撒勒湖邊，眾人擁擠他，要聽 神的道。

en: and he saw two boats by the lake, but the fishermen had gone out of them and were washing their nets.
zh: 他看見兩隻船停在湖邊，漁夫離開船洗網去了。

en: Getting into one of the boats, which was Simon's, he asked him to put out a little from the land. And he sat down and taught the people from the boat.
zh: 他上了西門的那一隻船，請他撐開，離岸不遠，就坐下，從船上教導眾人。

en: And when he had finished speaking, he said to Simon, "Put out into the deep and let down your nets for a catch."
zh: 講完了，就對西門說：“把船開到水深的地方，下網打魚！”

en: And Simon answered, "Master, we toiled all night and took nothing! But at your word I will let down the nets."
zh: 西門說：“主啊，我們整夜勞苦，毫無所得，不過，我願照你的話下網。”

en: And when they had done this, they enclosed a large number of fish, and their nets were breaking.
zh: 他們下了網，就圈住很多魚，網幾乎裂開，

en: They signaled to their partners in the other boat to come and help them. And they came and filled both the boats, so that they began to sink.
zh: 就招呼另外那隻船上的同伴來幫助，他們就來把兩隻船裝滿，甚至船要下沉。

en: But when Simon Peter saw it, he fell down at Jesus' knees, saying, "Depart from me, for I am a sinful man, O Lord."
zh: 西門．彼得看見這種情景，就俯伏在耶穌膝前，說：“主啊，離開我，因為我是個罪人。”

en: For he and all who were with him were astonished at the catch of fish that they had taken,
zh: 他和跟他在一起的人，因這網所打的魚，都十分驚駭。

en: and so also were James and John, sons of Zebedee, who were partners with Simon. And Jesus said to Simon, "Do not be afraid; from now on you will be catching men."
zh: 西門的夥伴，西庇太的兒子雅各、約翰也是這樣。耶穌對西門說：“不要怕！從今以後，你要作得人的漁夫了。”

en: And when they had brought their boats to land, they left everything and followed him.
zh: 他們把兩隻船攏了岸，撇下一切，跟從了耶穌。

en: While he was in one of the cities, there came a man full of leprosy. And when he saw Jesus, he fell on his face and begged him, "Lord, if you will, you can make me clean."
zh: 有一次，耶穌在一個城裡，突然有一個滿身痲風的人看見他，就把臉伏在地上，求他說：“主啊！如果你肯，必能使我潔淨。”

en: And Jesus stretched out his hand and touched him, saying, "I will; be clean." And immediately the leprosy left him.
zh: 耶穌伸手摸他，說：“我肯，你潔淨了吧！”痲風立刻離開了他。

en: And he charged him to tell no one, but "go and show yourself to the priest, and make an offering for your cleansing, as Moses commanded, for a proof to them."
zh: 耶穌囑咐他不可告訴任何人，“你只要去給祭司檢查，並且照著摩西所規定的，為你得潔淨獻祭，好向大家作證。”

en: But now even more the report about him went abroad, and great crowds gathered to hear him and to be healed of their infirmities.
zh: 但他的名聲卻越發傳揚出去，成群的人來聚集，要聽道，並且要使他們的疾病痊愈。

en: But he would withdraw to desolate places and pray.
zh: 耶穌卻退到曠野去禱告。

en: On one of those days, as he was teaching, Pharisees and teachers of the law were sitting there, who had come from every village of Galilee and Judea and from Jerusalem. And the power of the Lord was with him to heal.
zh: 有一天，耶穌正在教導人，法利賽人和律法教師也坐在那裡，他們是從加利利和猶太各鄉村，並耶路撒冷來的；主的能力與他同在，叫他能醫病。

en: And behold, some men were bringing on a bed a man who was paralyzed, and they were seeking to bring him in and lay him before Jesus,
zh: 有人用床抬著一個癱子，想送進去，放在耶穌跟前。

en: but finding no way to bring him in, because of the crowd, they went up on the roof and let him down with his bed through the tiles into the midst before Jesus.
zh: 因為人多，沒有辦法進去，就上了房頂，從瓦間把癱子和床往當中縋下去，正在耶穌跟前。

en: And when he saw their faith, he said, "Man, your sins are forgiven you."
zh: 他看見他們的信心，就說：“朋友 ，你的罪赦了。”

en: And the scribes and the Pharisees began to question, saying, "Who is this who speaks blasphemies? Who can forgive sins but God alone?"
zh: 經學家和法利賽人就議論起來，說：“這人是誰，竟然說僭妄的話？除 神一位以外，誰能赦罪呢？”

en: When Jesus perceived their thoughts, he answered them, "Why do you question in your hearts?
zh: 耶穌知道他們的議論，就對他們說：“你們心裡為甚麼議論呢？

en: Which is easier, to say, 'Your sins are forgiven you,' or to say, 'Rise and walk'?
zh: 說：‘你的罪赦了’，或說：‘起來行走’，哪一樣容易呢？

en: But that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins"- he said to the man who was paralyzed- "I say to you, rise, pick up your bed and go home."
zh: 然而為了要你們知道，人子在地上有赦罪的權柄， 我吩咐你，起來，拿起你的床，回家去吧。”

en: And immediately he rose up before them and picked up what he had been lying on and went home, glorifying God.
zh: 那人立刻當眾起來，拿著他躺過的床，頌讚 神，回家去了。

en: And amazement seized them all, and they glorified God and were filled with awe, saying, "We have seen extraordinary things today."
zh: 眾人都驚奇，頌讚 神，並且十分懼怕，說：“我們今天看見了不平常的事。”

en: After this he went out and saw a tax collector named Levi, sitting at the tax booth. And he said to him, "Follow me."
zh: 事後，耶穌出去，看見一個稅吏，名叫利未，坐在稅關那裡，就對他說：“來跟從我！”

en: And leaving everything, he rose and followed him.
zh: 他就撇下一切，起來跟從了耶穌。

en: And Levi made him a great feast in his house, and there was a large company of tax collectors and others reclining at table with them.
zh: 利未在自己家裡，為他大擺筵席，有許多稅吏和別的人一起吃飯。

en: And the Pharisees and their scribes grumbled at his disciples, saying, "Why do you eat and drink with tax collectors and sinners?"
zh: 法利賽人和經學家埋怨他的門徒，說：“你們為甚麼跟稅吏和罪人一起吃喝呢？”

en: And Jesus answered them, "Those who are well have no need of a physician, but those who are sick.
zh: 耶穌回答：“健康的人不需要醫生，有病的人才需要。

en: I have not come to call the righteous but sinners to repentance."
zh: 我來不是要召義人，而是要召罪人悔改。”

en: And they said to him, "The disciples of John fast often and offer prayers, and so do the disciples of the Pharisees, but yours eat and drink."
zh: 他們說：“約翰的門徒常常禁食、祈禱，法利賽人的門徒也是這樣，而你的門徒卻又吃又喝。”

en: And Jesus said to them, "Can you make wedding guests fast while the bridegroom is with them?
zh: 耶穌說：“新郎跟賓客在一起的時候，你們怎麼可以叫賓客禁食呢？

en: The days will come when the bridegroom is taken away from them, and then they will fast in those days."
zh: 但日子到了，新郎要被取去，離開他們，那一天他們就要禁食了。”

en: He also told them a parable: "No one tears a piece from a new garment and puts it on an old garment. If he does, he will tear the new, and the piece from the new will not match the old.
zh: 他又對他們設個比喻說：“沒有人會從新衣服撕下一塊布，補在舊衣服上，如果這樣，不但新衣服撕破了，而且新撕下的布，也和舊的不調和。

en: And no one puts new wine into old wineskins. If he does, the new wine will burst the skins and it will be spilled, and the skins will be destroyed.
zh: 也沒有人會把新酒裝在舊皮袋裡；如果這樣，新酒就會把皮袋脹破，不但酒漏掉，皮袋也損壞了；

en: But new wine must be put into fresh wineskins.
zh: 人總是把新酒裝在新皮袋裡。

en: And no one after drinking old wine desires new, for he says, 'The old is good.'"
zh: 喝慣陳酒的人，就不想喝新酒，他總說陳的好。”
